design_philosophy.core_loop: Defeat waves of enemy warriors in each room to open the exit door and progress to the next level
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Attrition-based combat against escalating enemy variety and attack patterns
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Kung-fu master rescuing Princess Min-Min and recovering stolen treasures through martial prowess
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Room-clearing gate progression system requiring player to exhaust enemy spawns before advancing; emphasis on sustained combat endurance rather than narrative pacing

progression.unlock_mechanism: Defeat sufficient enemies in each room to open exit door
replayability_hooks: Bonus levels accessible during main progression
Varying enemy attack patterns and weapons

world_building_techniques: Princess kidnapping setup establishes rescue mission framing
Treasure recovery objective creates collectible context
Generic 'nameless land' setting provides minimal world specificity

faction_indicators: Enemy warriors as hostile forces with varying attack profiles
danger_signals: Escalating enemy variety indicates progression difficulty
Weapons variety among enemies signals tactical complexity
Room-clearing gate design creates pressure to engage all spawned threats
environmental_storytelling: Room layout enforces attrition-based engagement model
Door placement at top of room creates spatial progression goal
Treasure recovery implicit in level structure

sources_used: Wikipedia: Chinese Hero (video game) - Plot, Gameplay, Reception sections
